# Mumei

> Mumei (無銘) is a formal verification toolchain and proof-driven programming language. It verifies existing code (Python, Rust, Go, TypeScript), natural-language specifications, and `.mm` modules using Z3, proof certificates, and AI-agent workflows.

## What Mumei does

- Finds bugs and spec drift in existing code without requiring `.mm` migration
- Verifies `.mm` source files using Z3 SMT solver
- Emits LLVM IR, Rust/Python FFI bindings, and proof certificates
- Provides MCP tools for integration with Claude Code, Devin, and other AI agents
- Supports gradual migration from existing code to formally verified `.mm` modules

## Key links

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/mumei-lang/mumei)
- [GitHub Pages / Documentation](https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei)
- [Language Reference](https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei/docs/LANGUAGE)
- [Onboarding Guide](https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei/docs/ONBOARDING)
- [Architecture](https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei/docs/ARCHITECTURE)
- [MCP Integration](https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei/docs/MCP)
- [Roadmap](https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei/docs/ROADMAP)
- [Technical Paper](https://github.com/mumei-lang/mumei/tree/develop/paper)
- [Mumei Agent](https://github.com/mumei-lang/mumei-agent)

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://mumei-lang.github.io/mumei/install.sh | bash
# or: brew install mumei-lang/mumei/mumei
```
